sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution satisfying bid requirements of the City allowing the City to participate
in competitively bid contracts, authorizing the City Manager to enter into contract with
Stommel Inc. dba Lehr, for Hybrid Police Patrol vehicle supplies, equipment, and
installation services with a contract term from December 15, 2024 to December 15,
2026 for an amount not to exceed $400,000.00.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding in the amount of $400,000.00 will be available for FY 2025 in the baseline
Public Works Equipment Replacement Fund. Additional funding will be made available
and appropriated as needed in future fiscal year budgets.
sections.current_situation:
The Police Department replaces patrol vehicles on a routine basis. Police Patrol
vehicles require specialized equipment and installation. The contract with Stommel Inc.
dba Lehr will provide supplies, equipment, and installation services for twenty-one (21)
vehicles that have been previously purchased by the City. The City’s competitive
bidding requirements for contracting will be met utilizing the County of Orange contract
No. MA-080-23011506.
These replacement vehicles are needed by the City’s Police Department Patrol Division
in their work to provide public safety services in Berkeley. This contract will support the
City’s Strategic Plan Priority Project, advancing our goal to create a resilient, safe,
connected, and prepared city.
sections.background:
City Charter Article XI Section 67.2 allows the City to purchase goods without
undergoing a competitive bid process if the City utilizes pricing obtained by another
entity through a competitive process. On January 16, 2024, the County of Orange
entered into a contract for Patrol Vehicle Equipment, Supplies, and Equipment

Internal
Contract: Stommel Inc dba Lehr for Hybrid Police Patrol CONSENT CALENDAR
Vehicles Supplies/Equipment and Installation Services December 10, 2024
Installation Services, contract No MA-080-23011506. Attachment A, Section 15 states
that this contract will be extended to other California local and state governmental
entities wishing to use the pre-negotiated prices and terms of this contract.
Throughout the year, the Department of Public Works purchases vehicles and
equipment for the City’s operating departments. These vehicles require specialized
supplies/equipment and installation. This is paid for through the Equipment
Replacement fund. If a purchase will exceed $50,000, the Department of Finance,
General Services Division solicits bids or “piggybacks” off competitively bid contacts to
ensure the City’s departments receive the best pricing.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Public Works Equipment Maintenance endeavors to procure the most fuel-efficient
vehicles and equipment that are suitable for the required tasks. Equipment Maintenance
also researches the feasibility and availability of fully electric vehicles and hybrid-electric
powertrains to support the City’s transition away from fossil fuels toward an all-electric
fleet, where technically feasible.
sections.rationale:
Hybrid Police patrol vehicles have been overdue for replacement and have been
previously purchased. The majority of current police patrol vehicles chosen for
replacement is over eight (8) years old and the oldest vehicle is fifteen (15) years old.
The majority of these vehicles were purchased in 2016 with the replacement vehicles
been purchased in 2023. Due to manufacturing delays, the replacement vehicles have
been only recently available for delivery.
These vehicles require specialized supplies, equipment, and installation to ensure that
they meet public safety and operational requirements. A contract is needed to secure
additional supplies and installation services for these vehicles for the City. Piggybacking
on pricing obtained through another public agency’s competitive bidding process
ensures that the City is receiving the most competitive pricing.
sections.alternatives:
None. The previously purchased vehicles require specialized supplies, equipment, and
installation to meet operational requirements. City staff do not have the capacity for the
upfitting of twenty-one vehicles.
CONTACT PERSON
Joy Brown, Operational Manager, (510) 981-6629
Sara Rubio, Associate Management Analyst, (510) 981-5372
Marcus Dinkins, Police Annuitant, (510) 981- 5871
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
Internal
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: STOMMEL INC, DBA LEHR, HYBRID POLICE PATROL VEHICLE
SUPPLIES/EQUIPMENT INSTALLATION SERVICES
WHEREAS, vehicle supplies/equipment and installation services are needed by the City
Police Department in their work to provide public safety services in Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, twenty-one (21) hybrid Police patrol vehicles that have been previously
purchased need specialized supplies/equipment and installation services to meet public
safety and operational requirements; and
WHEREAS, City Charter XI Section 67.2 allows the City to purchase goods without
undergoing a competitive bid process if the City uses pricing obtained by another entity
through a competitive bid process; and
WHEREAS, County of Orange contract bid procedures satisfy the procurement
requirements of the City of Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, On January 16, 2024, the County of Orange entered into a contract for Patrol
Vehicle Equipment, Supplies, and Equipment Installation Services, contract No MA-080-
23011506. Attachment A, Section 15 states that this contract will be extended to other
California local and state governmental entities wishing to use the pre-negotiated prices
and terms of this contract; and
WHEREAS, funds are available in the FY 2025 Equipment Replacement baseline budget
and will be appropriated in future fiscal years.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorize to execute a contract for Hybrid Police Patrol vehicle supplies,
equipment and installation services with Stommel Inc. dba Lehr for a contract term from
December 15, 2024 to December 15, 2026 in the amount not to exceed $400,000.00.
